Parse.definition: Frontend message that asks the server to prepare a (possibly named) SQL statement. Establishes the named prepared statement on the server.

ParseComplete.definition: Backend message acknowledging that a Parse succeeded. The named prepared statement is now available.

Bind.definition: Frontend message that binds a prepared statement to a portal with concrete parameter values and result format codes.

BindComplete.definition: Backend message acknowledging that a Bind succeeded. The portal is created and ready to be executed.

Execute.definition: Frontend message that runs the SQL behind a portal and streams back rows. Distinct from Sync.

Describe.definition: Frontend message asking the server to describe a statement (parameter types) or a portal (column types).

Sync.definition: Frontend message that closes a batch of extended-query commands. The server responds with ReadyForQuery and resyncs error state.

Query.definition: Frontend simple-query message: a single SQL string. Server runs it and streams back row descriptions, data, and CommandComplete.

Terminate.definition: Frontend message that gracefully closes the connection. No reply expected.

StartupMessage.definition: First message a frontend sends after connecting. Carries protocol version and the parameter list (user, database, application_name, ...).

SSLRequest.definition: Frontend pseudo-message sent before StartupMessage to ask whether the server accepts TLS. Server replies with a single byte (S = yes, N = no).

SSLResponse.definition: Backend single-byte reply to SSLRequest: 'S' = TLS accepted (client should now begin TLS handshake), 'N' = TLS not supported (client should either give up or continue in cleartext).

GSSENCRequest.definition: Frontend pseudo-message asking whether the server accepts GSSAPI encryption. Same 8-byte shape as SSLRequest, request code 80877104. Server replies with a single byte (G = yes, N = no).

GSSENCResponse.definition: Backend single-byte reply to GSSENCRequest: 'G' = GSSAPI accepted, 'N' = GSSAPI not supported (client continues without GSS encryption).

CancelRequest.definition: Frontend message sent on a fresh TCP connection to cancel an in-flight query on another connection. Carries the (pid, secret) pair the server sent earlier in BackendKeyData.

ReadyForQuery.definition: Backend message indicating the server is idle and the transaction status: I (idle), T (in a transaction), E (in a failed transaction).

RowDescription.definition: Backend message that announces the schema of upcoming DataRow messages: column names, table OID, type OID, format code (0 = text, 1 = binary).

DataRow.definition: Backend message carrying one row of values, one entry per column described by the preceding RowDescription.

CommandComplete.definition: Backend message marking the end of a successful statement, with a command tag like 'SELECT 3' or 'INSERT 0 1'.

ErrorResponse.definition: Backend message reporting an error. Carries fields keyed by single-letter codes (S=severity, C=SQLSTATE, M=message, D=detail, ...).

NoticeResponse.definition: Backend message reporting a non-fatal notice (warning, info, ...). Same field layout as ErrorResponse.

ParameterStatus.definition: Backend broadcast of a server-side setting (e.g. server_version, client_encoding). Sent unprompted on startup and on change.

ParameterDescription.definition: Backend response to a Describe-statement request, listing the OID of each parameter the prepared statement expects.

NoData.definition: Backend reply to a Describe when the statement or portal will not produce any rows (e.g. DML without RETURNING).

BackendKeyData.definition: Backend message sent at startup carrying the server's process ID and a secret cancellation key. Needed to send a CancelRequest later.

AuthenticationOK.definition: Backend message confirming authentication succeeded. The server then sends ParameterStatus, BackendKeyData, and ReadyForQuery.

AuthenticationMD5Password.definition: Backend challenge asking the client to send a hashed (MD5 + salt) password. Legacy mechanism, superseded by SCRAM in modern Postgres.

AuthenticationSASL.definition: Backend message initiating SASL authentication, listing the SASL mechanisms (e.g. SCRAM-SHA-256) the server supports.

AuthenticationSASLContinue.definition: Backend mid-handshake message carrying the SASL server-challenge for the next client step.

AuthenticationSASLFinal.definition: Backend final SASL message carrying the server's proof. After this, AuthenticationOK follows.

SASLInitialResponse.definition: Frontend response to AuthenticationSASL, carrying the chosen mechanism name and the initial SASL client-first message.

SASLResponse.definition: Frontend follow-up SASL message answering AuthenticationSASLContinue.

SCRAM.definition: Salted Challenge Response Authentication Mechanism. The default password authentication in modern Postgres (RFC 5802).

OID.definition: Object identifier: a 4-byte integer Postgres uses internally to identify a type, table, function, etc. Common type OIDs: 23 = int4, 25 = text, 1043 = varchar.

Portal.definition: A server-side cursor created by Bind, holding a prepared statement together with concrete parameter values. Read with Execute.

extended query.definition: Multi-step query protocol (Parse -> Bind -> Describe -> Execute -> Sync) that allows prepared statements, parameter binding, and pipelining.

simple query.definition: Single-message Query protocol: one SQL string in, one full result stream out, no parameters, no preparation.
